assertions.description: Iterator.concat is exposed as a static method on the global Iterator constructor.
Iterator.concat() with no arguments returns an iterator that yields nothing.
Iterator.concat accepts iterator helpers and arrays interchangeably.
Iterator.concat throws a TypeError when an argument is not iterable.
The value returned from Iterator.concat has Iterator.prototype on its chain (so chaining .map/.filter works).
If one source throws during iteration, Iterator.concat propagates the error and does not pull subsequent sources.
assertions.test: (()=>{let laterPulls=0;const values=[];function* first(){yield 'a';}function* middle(){yield 'b';throw new Error('boom');}function* later(){laterPulls++;yield 'c';}try{for(const value of Iterator.concat(first(),middle(),later()))values.push(value);return false;}catch(error){return error.message==='boom'&&values.join('')==='ab'&&laterPulls===0;}})()

assertions.description: Iterator.concat can consume String objects as iterable object inputs and yield their characters in order.
